L. 105–33, title IV, § 4018111 Stat. 346Aug. 5, 1997“(a)Demonstration Project.—“(1).—EstablishmentThe Secretary shall implement a demonstration project (in this section referred to as the ‘project’) for the purpose of evaluating the use of a third-party contractor to conduct the Medicare+Choice plan enrollment and disenrollment functions, as described in part C of title XVIII of the Social Security Act [ et seq.] (as added by section 4001 of this Act), in an area.42 U.S.C. 1395w–21“(2).—ConsultationBefore implementing the project under this section, the Secretary shall consult with affected parties on—“(A)the design of the project;“(B)the selection criteria for the third-party contractor; and“(C)the establishment of performance standards, as described in paragraph (3).“(3)Performance standards.—“(A).—In generalThe Secretary shall establish performance standards for the accuracy and timeliness of the Medicare+Choice plan enrollment and disenrollment functions performed by the third-party contractor.“(B).—NoncomplianceIn the event that the third-party contractor is not in substantial compliance with the performance standards established under subparagraph (A), such enrollment and disenrollment functions shall be performed by the Medicare+Choice plan until the Secretary appoints a new third-party contractor.“(b).—Report to CongressThe Secretary shall periodically report to Congress on the progress of the project conducted pursuant to this section.“(c).—Waiver AuthorityThe Secretary shall waive compliance with the requirements of part C of title XVIII of the Social Security Act [ et seq.] (as amended by section 4001 of this Act) to such extent and for such period as the Secretary determines is necessary to conduct the project.42 U.S.C. 1395w–21“(d).—DurationA demonstration project under this section shall be conducted for a 3-year period.“(e).—Separate From Other Demonstration Proj­ectsA project implemented by the Secretary under this section shall not be conducted in conjunction with any other demonstration project.”

children.heading: Choice of medicare benefits through Medicare+Choice plans
children.label: a Choice of medicare benefits through Medicare+Choice plans
A
children.text: through the original medicare fee-for-service program under parts A and B, or

children.label: B
children.text: through enrollment in a Medicare+Choice plan under this part,

children.heading: Types of Medicare+Choice plans that may be available
children.label: 2 Types of Medicare+Choice plans that may be available

children.heading: Coordinated care plans (including regional plans)
children.label: A Coordinated care plans (including regional plans)

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Coordinated care plans which provide health care services, including but not limited to health maintenance organization plans (with or without point of service options), plans offered by provider-sponsored organizations (as defined in ), and regional or local preferred provider organization plans (including MA regional plans).section 1395w–25(d) of this title

children.heading: Specialized MA plans for special needs individuals
children.label: ii Specialized MA plans for special needs individuals
children.text: Specialized MA plans for special needs individuals (as defined in ) may be any type of coordinated care plan.section 1395w–28(b)(6) of this title

children.heading: Combination of MSA plan and contributions to Medicare+Choice MSA
children.label: B Combination of MSA plan and contributions to Medicare+Choice MSA
children.text: An MSA plan, as defined in , and a contribution into a Medicare+Choice medical savings account (MSA).section 1395w–28(b)(3) of this title

children.heading: Private fee-for-service plans
children.label: C Private fee-for-service plans
children.text: A Medicare+Choice private fee-for-service plan, as defined in .section 1395w–28(b)(2) of this title

children.heading: Medicare+Choice eligible individual
children.label: 3 Medicare+Choice eligible individual
children.text: In this subchapter, the term “Medicare+Choice eligible individual” means an individual who is entitled to benefits under part A and enrolled under part B.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as the Secretary may otherwise provide and except as provided in subparagraph (C), an individual is eligible to elect a Medicare+Choice plan offered by a Medicare+Choice organization only if the plan serves the geographic area in which the individual resides.

children.heading: Continuation of enrollment permitted
children.label: B Continuation of enrollment permitted
children.text: Pursuant to rules specified by the Secretary, the Secretary shall provide that an MA local plan may offer to all individuals residing in a geographic area the option to continue enrollment in the plan, notwithstanding that the individual no longer resides in the service area of the plan, so long as the plan provides that individuals exercising this option have, as part of the benefits under the original medicare fee-for-service program option, reasonable access within that geographic area to the full range of basic benefits, subject to reasonable cost sharing liability in obtaining such benefits.

children.heading: Continuation of enrollment permitted where service changed
children.label: C Continuation of enrollment permitted where service changed
i
children.text: the enrollee agrees to receive the full range of basic benefits (excluding emergency and urgently needed care) exclusively at facilities designated by the organization within the plan service area; and

children.label: ii
children.text: there is no other Medicare+Choice plan offered in the area in which the enrollee resides at the time of the organization’s election.

children.heading: Special rule for certain individuals covered under FEHBP or eligible for veterans or military health benefits
children.label: 2 Special rule for certain individuals covered under FEHBP or eligible for veterans or military health benefits

children.heading: FEHBP
children.label: A FEHBP
children.text: An individual who is enrolled in a health benefit plan under chapter 89 of title 5 is not eligible to enroll in an MSA plan until such time as the Director of the Office of Management and Budget certifies to the Secretary that the Office of Personnel Management has adopted policies which will ensure that the enrollment of such individuals in such plans will not result in increased expenditures for the Federal Government for health benefit plans under such chapter.

children.heading: VA and DOD
children.label: B VA and DOD
children.text: The Secretary may apply rules similar to the rules described in subparagraph (A) in the case of individuals who are eligible for health care benefits under chapter 55 of title 10 or under chapter 17 of title 38.

children.heading: Limitation on eligibility of qualified medicare beneficiaries and other medicaid beneficiaries to enroll in an MSA plan
children.label: 3 Limitation on eligibility of qualified medicare beneficiaries and other medicaid beneficiaries to enroll in an MSA plan
children.text: An individual who is a qualified medicare beneficiary (as defined in ), a qualified disabled and working individual (described in ), an individual described in , or otherwise entitled to medicare cost-sharing under a State plan under subchapter XIX is not eligible to enroll in an MSA plan.section 1396d(p)(1) of this titlesection 1396d(s) of this titlesection 1396a(a)(10)(E)(iii) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Under rules established by the Secretary, an individual is not eligible to enroll (or continue enrollment) in an MSA plan for a year unless the individual provides assurances satisfactory to the Secretary that the individual will reside in the United States for at least 183 days during the year.

children.heading: Evaluation
children.label: B Evaluation
children.text: The Secretary shall regularly evaluate the impact of permitting enrollment in MSA plans under this part on selection (including adverse selection), use of preventive care, access to care, and the financial status of the Trust Funds under this subchapter.

children.heading: Reports
children.label: C Reports
children.text: The Secretary shall submit to Congress periodic reports on the numbers of individuals enrolled in such plans and on the evaluation being conducted under subparagraph (B).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a process through which elections described in subsection (a) are made and changed, including the form and manner in which such elections are made and changed. Subject to paragraph (4), such elections shall be made or changed only during coverage election periods specified under subsection (e) and shall become effective as provided in subsection (f).

children.heading: Enrollment
children.label: A Enrollment
children.text: Such process shall permit an individual who wishes to elect a Medicare+Choice plan offered by a Medicare+Choice organization to make such election through the filing of an appropriate election form with the organization.

children.heading: Disenrollment
children.label: B Disenrollment
children.text: Such process shall permit an individual, who has elected a Medicare+Choice plan offered by a Medicare+Choice organization and who wishes to terminate such election, to terminate such election through the filing of an appropriate election form with the organization.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to clause (ii), an individual who fails to make an election during an initial election period under subsection (e)(1) is deemed to have chosen the original medicare fee-for-service program option.

children.heading: Seamless continuation of coverage
children.label: ii Seamless continuation of coverage
children.text: The Secretary may establish procedures under which an individual who is enrolled in a health plan (other than Medicare+Choice plan) offered by a Medicare+Choice organization at the time of the initial election period and who fails to elect to receive coverage other than through the organization is deemed to have elected the Medicare+Choice plan offered by the organization (or, if the organization offers more than one such plan, such plan or plans as the Secretary identifies under such procedures).

children.label: i
children.text: the individual changes the election under this section, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the Medicare+Choice plan with respect to which such election is in effect is discontinued or, subject to subsection (b)(1)(B), no longer serves the area in which the individual resides.

children.heading: Deemed enrollment relating to converted reasonable cost reimbursement contracts
children.label: 4 Deemed enrollment relating to converted reasonable cost reimbursement contracts
i
children.text: the individual is enrolled in a reasonable cost reimbursement contract under in the previous plan year;section 1395mm(h) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: such reasonable cost reimbursement contract was extended or renewed for the last reasonable cost reimbursement contract year of the contract (as described in subclause (I) of ) pursuant to such section;section 1395mm(h)(5)(C)(iv) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: the eligible organization that is offering such reasonable cost reimbursement contract provided the notice described in subclause (III) of such section that the contract was to be converted;

children.label: I
children.text: is the plan that was converted from the reasonable cost reimbursement contract described in clause (iii);

children.label: II
children.text: is offered by the same entity (or an organization affiliated with such entity that has a common ownership interest of control) that entered into such contract; and

children.label: III
children.text: is offered in the service area where the individual resides;

children.label: v
children.text: in the case of reasonable cost reimbursement contracts that provide coverage under parts A and B (and, to the extent the Secretary determines it to be feasible, contracts that provide only part B coverage), the difference between the estimated individual costs (as determined applicable by the Secretary) for the applicable MA plan and such costs for the predecessor cost plan does not exceed a threshold established by the Secretary; and

children.label: I
children.text: provides coverage for enrollees transitioning from the converted reasonable cost reimbursement contract to such plan to maintain current providers of services and suppliers and course of treatment at the time of enrollment for a period of at least 90 days after enrollment; and

children.label: II
children.text: during such period, pays such providers of services and suppliers for items and services furnished to the enrollee an amount that is not less than the amount of payment applicable for such items and services under the original Medicare fee-for-service program under parts A and B.

children.heading: Without prescription drug coverage
children.label: i Without prescription drug coverage
children.text: An MA eligible individual described in this clause, with respect to a plan year, is an MA eligible individual who is enrolled in a reasonable cost reimbursement contract under in the previous plan year and who is not, for such previous plan year, enrolled in a prescription drug plan under part D, including coverage under .section 1395mm(h) of this titlesection 1395w–132 of this title

children.label: I
children.text: through such contract; or

children.label: II
children.text: through a prescription drug plan, if the sponsor of such plan is the same entity (or an organization affiliated with such entity) that entered into such contract.

children.label: i
children.text: subparagraph (B)(i), an MA plan that is not an MA–PD plan; and

children.label: ii
children.text: subparagraph (B)(ii), an MA–PD plan.

children.heading: Identification and notification of deemed individuals
children.label: D Identification and notification of deemed individuals
children.text: Not later than 45 days before the first day of the annual, coordinated election period under subsection (e)(3) for plan years beginning on or after , the Secretary shall identify and notify the individuals who will be subject to deemed elections under subparagraph (A) on the first day of such period.January 1, 2017

children.heading: Providing information to promote informed choice
children.label: d Providing information to promote informed choice

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall provide for activities under this subsection to broadly disseminate information to medicare beneficiaries (and prospective medicare beneficiaries) on the coverage options provided under this section in order to promote an active, informed selection among such options.

children.heading: General information
children.label: i General information
children.text: The general information described in paragraph (3).

children.heading: List of plans and comparison of plan options
children.label: ii List of plans and comparison of plan options
children.text: A list identifying the Medicare+Choice plans that are (or will be) available to residents of the area and information described in paragraph (4) concerning such plans. Such information shall be presented in a comparative form.

children.heading: Additional information
children.label: iii Additional information
children.text: Any other information that the Secretary determines will assist the individual in making the election under this section.

children.heading: Notification to newly eligible Medicare Advantage eligible individuals
children.label: i Notification to newly eligible Medicare Advantage eligible individuals
children.text: To the extent practicable, the Secretary shall, not later than 30 days before the beginning of the initial Medicare+Choice enrollment period for an individual described in subsection (e)(1), mail to the individual the information described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Notification related to certain deemed elections
children.label: ii Notification related to certain deemed elections
I
children.text: a notification that such individual will, on such day, be deemed to have made an election with respect to such plan to receive benefits under this subchapter through an MA plan or MA–PD plan (and shall be enrolled in such plan) for the next plan year under subsection (c)(4)(A), but that the individual may make a different election during the annual, coordinated election period for such year;

children.label: II
children.text: the information described in subparagraph (A);

children.label: III
children.text: a description of the differences between such MA plan or MA–PD plan and the reasonable cost reimbursement contract in which the individual was most recently enrolled with respect to benefits covered under such plans, including cost-sharing, premiums, drug coverage, and provider networks;

children.label: IV
children.text: information about the special period for elections under subsection (e)(2)(F); and

children.label: V
children.text: other information the Secretary may specify.

children.heading: Form
children.label: C Form
children.text: The information disseminated under this paragraph shall be written and formatted using language that is easily understandable by medicare beneficiaries.

children.heading: Periodic updating
children.label: D Periodic updating
children.text: The information described in subparagraph (A) shall be updated on at least an annual basis to reflect changes in the availability of Medicare+Choice plans and the benefits and Medicare+Choice monthly basic and supplemental beneficiary premiums for such plans.

children.heading: Benefits under original medicare fee-for-service program option
children.label: A Benefits under original medicare fee-for-service program option
i
children.text: covered items and services,

children.label: ii
children.text: beneficiary cost sharing, such as deductibles, coinsurance, and copayment amounts, and

children.label: iii
children.text: any beneficiary liability for balance billing.

children.heading: Election procedures
children.label: B Election procedures
children.text: Information and instructions on how to exercise election options under this section.

children.heading: Rights
children.label: C Rights
children.text: A general description of procedural rights (including grievance and appeals procedures) of beneficiaries under the original medicare fee-for-service program and the Medicare+Choice program and the right to be protected against discrimination based on health status-related factors under .section 1395w–22(b) of this title

children.heading: Information on medigap and medicare select
children.label: D Information on medigap and medicare select
children.text: A general description of the benefits, enrollment rights, and other requirements applicable to medicare supplemental policies under and provisions relating to medicare select policies described in .section 1395ss of this titlesection 1395ss(t) of this title

children.heading: Potential for contract termination
children.label: E Potential for contract termination
children.text: The fact that a Medicare+Choice organization may terminate its contract, refuse to renew its contract, or reduce the service area included in its contract, under this part, and the effect of such a termination, nonrenewal, or service area reduction may have on individuals enrolled with the Medicare+Choice plan under this part.

children.heading: Catastrophic coverage and single deductible
children.label: F Catastrophic coverage and single deductible
children.text: In the case of an MA regional plan, a description of the catastrophic coverage and single deductible applicable under the plan.

children.label: i
children.text: Covered items and services beyond those provided under the original medicare fee-for-service program.

children.label: ii
children.text: Any beneficiary cost sharing, including information on the single deductible (if applicable) under .section 1395w–27a(b)(1) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: Any maximum limitations on out-of-pocket expenses.

children.label: iv
children.text: In the case of an MSA plan, differences in cost sharing, premiums, and balance billing under such a plan compared to under other Medicare+Choice plans.

children.label: v
children.text: In the case of a Medicare+Choice private fee-for-service plan, differences in cost sharing, premiums, and balance billing under such a plan compared to under other Medicare+Choice plans.

children.label: vi
children.text: The extent to which an enrollee may obtain benefits through out-of-network health care providers.

children.label: vii
children.text: The extent to which an enrollee may select among in-network providers and the types of providers participating in the plan’s network.

children.label: viii
children.text: The organization’s coverage of emergency and urgently needed care.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The monthly amount of the premium charged to an individual.

children.heading: Reductions
children.label: ii Reductions
children.text: The reduction in part B premiums, if any.

children.heading: Service area
children.label: C Service area
children.text: The service area of the plan.

children.label: i
children.text: disenrollment rates for medicare enrollees electing to receive benefits through the plan for the previous 2 years (excluding disenrollment due to death or moving outside the plan’s service area),

children.label: ii
children.text: information on medicare enrollee satisfaction,

children.label: iii
children.text: information on health outcomes, and

children.label: iv
children.text: the recent record regarding compliance of the plan with requirements of this part (as determined by the Secretary).

children.heading: Supplemental benefits
children.label: E Supplemental benefits
children.text: Supplemental health care benefits, including any reductions in cost-sharing under and the terms and conditions (including premiums) for such benefits.section 1395w–22(a)(3) of this title

children.heading: Provider directory
children.label: F Provider directory
children.text: Beginning with plan years beginning on or after , in the case of a specified MA plan (as defined in ), the accuracy score of the plan’s provider directory (as reported under ) listed prominently on the plan’s provider directory.January 1, 2029section 1395w–22(c)(3)(C) of this titlesection 1395w–27(e)(6)(A)(ii) of this title

children.heading: Maintaining a toll-free number and Internet site
children.label: 5 Maintaining a toll-free number and Internet site
children.text: The Secretary shall maintain a toll-free number for inquiries regarding Medicare+Choice options and the operation of this part in all areas in which Medicare+Choice plans are offered and an Internet site through which individuals may electronically obtain information on such options and Medicare+Choice plans.

children.heading: Use of non-Federal entities
children.label: 6 Use of non-Federal entities
children.text: The Secretary may enter into contracts with non-Federal entities to carry out activities under this subsection.

children.heading: Provision of information
children.label: 7 Provision of information
children.text: A Medicare+Choice organization shall provide the Secretary with such information on the organization and each Medicare+Choice plan it offers as may be required for the preparation of the information referred to in paragraph (2)(A).

children.heading: Initial choice upon eligibility to make election if Medicare+Choice plans available to individual
children.label: 1 Initial choice upon eligibility to make election if Medicare+Choice plans available to individual
children.text: If, at the time an individual first becomes entitled to benefits under part A and enrolled under part B, there is one or more Medicare+Choice plans offered in the area in which the individual resides, the individual shall make the election under this section during a period specified by the Secretary such that if the individual elects a Medicare+Choice plan during the period, coverage under the plan becomes effective as of the first date on which the individual may receive such coverage. If any portion of an individual’s initial enrollment period under part B occurs after the end of the annual, coordinated election period described in paragraph (3)(B)(iii), the initial enrollment period under this part shall further extend through the end of the individual’s initial enrollment period under part B.

children.heading: Continuous open enrollment and disenrollment through 2005
children.label: A Continuous open enrollment and disenrollment through 2005
children.text: At any time during the period beginning , and ending on , a Medicare+Choice eligible individual may change the election under subsection (a)(1).January 1, 1998December 31, 2005

children.heading: Continuous open enrollment and disenrollment for first 6 months during 2006
children.label: B Continuous open enrollment and disenrollment for first 6 months during 2006

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to clause (ii), subparagraph (C)(iii), and subparagraph (D), at any time during the first 6 months of 2006, or, if the individual first becomes a Medicare+Choice eligible individual during 2006, during the first 6 months during 2006 in which the individual is a Medicare+Choice eligible individual, a Medicare+Choice eligible individual may change the election under subsection (a)(1).1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Limitation of one change
children.label: ii Limitation of one change
children.text: An individual may exercise the right under clause (i) only once. The limitation under this clause shall not apply to changes in elections effected during an annual, coordinated election period under paragraph (3) or during a special enrollment period under the first sentence of paragraph (4).

children.heading: Annual 45-day period from 2011 through 2018 for disenrollment from MA plans to elect to receive benefits under the original Medicare fee-for-service program
children.label: C Annual 45-day period from 2011 through 2018 for disenrollment from MA plans to elect to receive benefits under the original Medicare fee-for-service program
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (D), at any time during the first 45 days of a year (beginning with 2011 and ending with 2018), an individual who is enrolled in a Medicare Advantage plan may change the election under subsection (a)(1), but only with respect to coverage under the original medicare fee-for-service program under parts A and B, and may elect qualified prescription drug coverage in accordance with .section 1395w–101 of this title

children.heading: Continuous open enrollment for institutionalized individuals
children.label: D Continuous open enrollment for institutionalized individuals
i
children.text: to enroll in a Medicare+Choice plan; or

children.label: ii
children.text: to change the Medicare+Choice plan in which the individual is enrolled.

children.heading: Limited continuous open enrollment of original fee-for-service enrollees in medicare advantage non-prescription drug plans
children.label: E Limited continuous open enrollment of original fee-for-service enrollees in medicare advantage non-prescription drug plans

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: On any date during the period beginning on , and ending on , on which a Medicare Advantage eligible individual is an unenrolled fee-for-service individual (as defined in clause (ii)), the individual may elect under subsection (a)(1) to enroll in a Medicare Advantage plan that is not an MA–PD plan.January 1, 2007July 31, 2007

children.label: I
children.text: is receiving benefits under this subchapter through enrollment in the original medicare fee-for-service program under parts A and B;

children.label: II
children.text: is not enrolled in an MA plan on such date; and

children.label: III
children.text: as of such date is not otherwise eligible to elect to enroll in an MA plan.

children.heading: Limitation of one change during the applicable period
children.label: iii Limitation of one change during the applicable period
children.text: An individual may exercise the right under clause (i) only once during the period described in such clause.

children.heading: No effect on coverage under a prescription drug plan
children.label: iv No effect on coverage under a prescription drug plan
I
children.text: who is enrolled in a prescription drug plan under part D, to disenroll from such plan or to enroll in a different prescription drug plan; or

children.label: II
children.text: who is not enrolled in a prescription drug plan, to enroll in such a plan.

children.heading: Special period for certain deemed elections
children.label: F Special period for certain deemed elections

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: At any time during the period beginning after the last day of the annual, coordinated election period under paragraph (3) in which an individual is deemed to have elected to enroll in an MA plan or MA–PD plan under subsection (c)(4) and ending on the last day of February of the first plan year for which the individual is enrolled in such plan, such individual may change the election under subsection (a)(1) (including changing the MA plan or MA–PD plan in which the individual is enrolled).

children.heading: Limitation of one change
children.label: ii Limitation of one change
children.text: An individual may exercise the right under clause (i) only once during the applicable period described in such clause. The limitation under this clause shall not apply to changes in elections effected during an annual, coordinated election period under paragraph (3) or during a special enrollment period under paragraph (4).

children.heading: Continuous open enrollment and disenrollment for first 3 months in 2016 and subsequent years
children.label: G Continuous open enrollment and disenrollment for first 3 months in 2016 and subsequent years
I
children.text: in the case of an MA eligible individual who is enrolled in an MA plan, at any time during the first 3 months of a year (beginning with 2019); or

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of an individual who first becomes an MA eligible individual during a year (beginning with 2019) and enrolls in an MA plan, during the first 3 months during such year in which the individual is an MA eligible individual;

children.heading: Limitation of one change during open enrollment period each year
children.label: ii Limitation of one change during open enrollment period each year
children.text: An individual may change the election pursuant to clause (i) only once during the applicable 3-month period described in such clause in each year. The limitation under this clause shall not apply to changes in elections effected during an annual, coordinated election period under paragraph (3) or during a special enrollment period under paragraph (4).

children.heading: Limited application to part D
children.label: iii Limited application to part D
children.text: Clauses (i) and (ii) of this subparagraph shall only apply with respect to changes in enrollment in a prescription drug plan under part D in the case of an individual who, previous to such change in enrollment, is enrolled in a Medicare Advantage plan.

children.heading: Limitations on marketing
children.label: iv Limitations on marketing
children.text: Pursuant to subsection (j), no unsolicited marketing or marketing materials may be sent to an individual described in clause (i) during the continuous open enrollment and disenrollment period established for the individual under such clause, notwithstanding marketing guidelines established by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (5), each individual who is eligible to make an election under this section may change such election during an annual, coordinated election period.

children.label: i
children.text: with respect to a year before 2002, the month of November before such year;

children.label: ii
children.text: with respect to 2002, 2003, 2004, and 2005, the period beginning on November 15 and ending on December 31 of the year before such year;

children.label: iii
children.text: with respect to 2006, the period beginning on , and ending on ;November 15, 2005May 15, 2006

children.label: iv
children.text: with respect to 2007, 2008, 2009, and 2010, the period beginning on November 15 and ending on December 31 of the year before such year; and

children.label: v
children.text: with respect to 2012 and succeeding years, the period beginning on October 15 and ending on December 7 of the year before such year.

children.heading: Medicare+Choice health information fairs
children.label: C Medicare+Choice health information fairs
children.text: During the fall season of each year (beginning with 1999) and during the period described in subparagraph (B)(iii), in conjunction with the annual coordinated election period defined in subparagraph (B), the Secretary shall provide for a nationally coordinated educational and publicity campaign to inform Medicare+Choice eligible individuals about Medicare+Choice plans and the election process provided under this section.

children.heading: Special information campaigns
children.label: D Special information campaigns
children.text: During November 1998 the Secretary shall provide for an educational and publicity campaign to inform Medicare+Choice eligible individuals about the availability of Medicare+Choice plans, and eligible organizations with risk-sharing contracts under , offered in different areas and the election process provided under this section. During the period described in subparagraph (B)(iii), the Secretary shall provide for an educational and publicity campaign to inform MA eligible individuals about the availability of MA plans (including MA–PD plans) offered in different areas and the election process provided under this section.section 1395mm of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the certification of the organization or plan under this part has been terminated, or the organization or plan has notified the individual of an impending termination of such certification; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the organization has terminated or otherwise discontinued providing the plan in the area in which the individual resides, or has notified the individual of an impending termination or discontinuation of such plan;

children.label: B
children.text: the individual is no longer eligible to elect the plan because of a change in the individual’s place of residence or other change in circumstances (specified by the Secretary, but not including termination of the individual’s enrollment on the basis described in clause (i) or (ii) of subsection (g)(3)(B));

children.label: i
children.text: the organization offering the plan substantially violated a material provision of the organization’s contract under this part in relation to the individual (including the failure to provide an enrollee on a timely basis medically necessary care for which benefits are available under the plan or the failure to provide such covered care in accordance with applicable quality standards); or

children.label: ii
children.text: the organization (or an agent or other entity acting on the organization’s behalf) materially misrepresented the plan’s provisions in marketing the plan to the individual; or

children.label: D
children.text: the individual meets such other exceptional conditions as the Secretary may provide.

children.label: i
children.text: an initial open enrollment period described in paragraph (1), or

children.label: ii
children.text: an annual, coordinated election period described in paragraph (3)(B);

children.label: B
children.text: subject to subparagraph (C), may not discontinue an election of an MSA plan except during the periods described in clause (ii) or (iii) of subparagraph (A) and under the first sentence of paragraph (4); and

children.label: C
children.text: who elects an MSA plan during an annual, coordinated election period, and who never previously had elected such a plan, may revoke such election, in a manner determined by the Secretary, by not later than December 15 following the date of the election.

children.label: A
children.text: shall accept elections or changes to elections during the initial enrollment periods described in paragraph (1), during the period described in paragraph (2)(F), during the month of November 1998 and during the annual, coordinated election period under paragraph (3) for each subsequent year, and during special election periods described in the first sentence of paragraph (4); and

children.label: B
children.text: may accept other changes to elections at such other times as the organization provides.

children.heading: Effectiveness of elections and changes of elections
children.label: f Effectiveness of elections and changes of elections

children.heading: During initial coverage election period
children.label: 1 During initial coverage election period
children.text: An election of coverage made during the initial coverage election period under subsection (e)(1) shall take effect upon the date the individual becomes entitled to benefits under part A and enrolled under part B, except as the Secretary may provide (consistent with ) in order to prevent retroactive coverage.section 1395q of this title

children.heading: During continuous open enrollment periods
children.label: 2 During continuous open enrollment periods
children.text: An election or change of coverage made under subsection (e)(2) shall take effect with the first day of the first calendar month following the date on which the election or change is made.

children.heading: Annual, coordinated election period
children.label: 3 Annual, coordinated election period
children.text: An election or change of coverage made during an annual, coordinated election period (as defined in subsection (e)(3)(B), other than the period described in clause (iii) of such subsection) in a year shall take effect as of the first day of the following year.

children.heading: Other periods
children.label: 4 Other periods
children.text: An election or change of coverage made during any other period under subsection (e)(4) shall take effect in such manner as the Secretary provides in a manner consistent (to the extent practicable) with protecting continuity of health benefit coverage.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in this subsection, a Medicare+Choice organization shall provide that at any time during which elections are accepted under this section with respect to a Medicare+Choice plan offered by the organization, the organization will accept without restrictions individuals who are eligible to make such election.

children.label: A
children.text: first to such individuals as have elected the plan at the time of the determination, and

children.label: B
children.text: then to other such individuals in such a manner that does not discriminate, on a basis described in , among the individuals (who seek to elect the plan).section 1395w–22(b) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), a Medicare+ÐChoice organization may not for any reason terminate the election of any individual under this section for a Medicare+Choice plan it offers.

children.heading: Basis for termination of election
children.label: B Basis for termination of election
i
children.text: any Medicare+Choice monthly basic and supplemental beneficiary premiums required with respect to such plan are not paid on a timely basis (consistent with standards under that provide for a grace period for late payment of such premiums),section 1395w–26 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the individual has engaged in disruptive behavior (as specified in such standards), or

children.label: iii
children.text: the plan is terminated with respect to all individuals under this part in the area in which the individual resides.

children.heading: Terminations for cause
children.label: i Terminations for cause
children.text: Any individual whose election is terminated under clause (i) or (ii) of subparagraph (B) is deemed to have elected the original medicare fee-for-service program option described in subsection (a)(1)(A).

children.heading: Termination based on plan termination or service area reduction
children.label: ii Termination based on plan termination or service area reduction
children.text: Any individual whose election is terminated under subparagraph (B)(iii) shall have a special election period under subsection (e)(4)(A) in which to change coverage to coverage under another Medicare+Choice plan. Such an individual who fails to make an election during such period is deemed to have chosen to change coverage to the original medicare fee-for-service program option described in subsection (a)(1)(A).

children.heading: Organization obligation with respect to election forms
children.label: D Organization obligation with respect to election forms
children.text: Pursuant to a contract under , each Medicare+Choice organization receiving an election form under subsection (c)(2) shall transmit to the Secretary (at such time and in such manner as the Secretary may specify) a copy of such form or such other information respecting the election as the Secretary may specify.section 1395w–27 of this title

children.heading: Approval of marketing material and application forms
children.label: h Approval of marketing material and application forms
A
children.text: at least 45 days (or 10 days in the case described in paragraph (5)) before the date of distribution the organization has submitted the material or form to the Secretary for review, and

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary has not disapproved the distribution of such material or form.

children.heading: Review
children.label: 2 Review
children.text: The standards established under shall include guidelines for the review of any material or form submitted and under such guidelines the Secretary shall disapprove (or later require the correction of) such material or form if the material or form is materially inaccurate or misleading or otherwise makes a material misrepresentation.section 1395w–26 of this title

children.heading: Deemed approval (1-stop shopping)
children.label: 3 Deemed approval (1-stop shopping)
children.text: In the case of material or form that is submitted under paragraph (1)(A) to the Secretary or a regional office of the Department of Health and Human Services and the Secretary or the office has not disapproved the distribution of marketing material or form under paragraph (1)(B) with respect to a Medicare+Choice plan in an area, the Secretary is deemed not to have disapproved such distribution in all other areas covered by the plan and organization except with regard to that portion of such material or form that is specific only to an area involved.

children.label: A
children.text: shall not permit a Medicare+Choice organization to provide for, subject to subsection (j)(2)(C), cash, gifts, prizes, or other monetary rebates as an inducement for enrollment or otherwise;

children.label: B
children.text: may include a prohibition against a Medicare+Choice organization (or agent of such an organization) completing any portion of any election form used to carry out elections under this section on behalf of any individual;

children.label: C
children.text: shall not permit a Medicare Advantage organization (or the agents, brokers, and other third parties representing such organization) to conduct the prohibited activities described in subsection (j)(1); and

children.label: D
children.text: shall only permit a Medicare Advantage organization (and the agents, brokers, and other third parties representing such organization) to conduct the activities described in subsection (j)(2) in accordance with the limitations established under such subsection.

children.heading: Special treatment of marketing material following model marketing language
children.label: 5 Special treatment of marketing material following model marketing language
children.text: In the case of marketing material of an organization that uses, without modification, proposed model language specified by the Secretary, the period specified in paragraph (1)(A) shall be reduced from 45 days to 10 days.

children.heading: Required inclusion of plan type in plan name
children.label: 6 Required inclusion of plan type in plan name
children.text: For plan years beginning on or after , a Medicare Advantage organization must ensure that the name of each Medicare Advantage plan offered by the Medicare Advantage organization includes the plan type of the plan (using standard terminology developed by the Secretary).January 1, 2010

children.heading: Strengthening the ability of States to act in collaboration with the Secretary to address fraudulent or inappropriate marketing practices
children.label: 7 Strengthening the ability of States to act in collaboration with the Secretary to address fraudulent or inappropriate marketing practices

children.heading: Appointment of agents and brokers
children.label: A Appointment of agents and brokers
i
children.text: only use agents and brokers who have been licensed under State law to sell Medicare Advantage plans offered by the Medicare Advantage organization;

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case where a State has a State appointment law, abide by such law; and

children.label: iii
children.text: report to the applicable State the termination of any such agent or broker, including the reasons for such termination (as required under applicable State law).

children.heading: Compliance with State information requests
children.label: B Compliance with State information requests
children.text: Each Medicare Advantage organization shall comply in a timely manner with any request by a State for information regarding the performance of a licensed agent, broker, or other third party representing the Medicare Advantage organization as part of an investigation by the State into the conduct of the agent, broker, or other third party.

children.heading: Effect of election of Medicare+Choice plan option
children.label: i Effect of election of Medicare+Choice plan option

children.heading: Payments to organizations
children.label: 1 Payments to organizations
children.text: Subject to sections 1395w–22(a)(5), 1395w–23(a)(4), 1395w–23(g), 1395w–23(h), 1395ww(d)(11), 1395ww(h)(3)(D), and 1395w–23(m) of this title, payments under a contract with a Medicare+Choice organization under with respect to an individual electing a Medicare+Choice plan offered by the organization shall be instead of the amounts which (in the absence of the contract) would otherwise be payable under parts A and B for items and services furnished to the individual.section 1395w–23(a) of this title

children.heading: Only organization entitled to payment
children.label: 2 Only organization entitled to payment
children.text: Subject to sections 1395w–23(a)(4), 1395w–23(e), 1395w–23(g), 1395w–23(h), 1395w–27(f)(2), 1395w–27a(h), 1395ww(d)(11), and 1395ww(h)(3)(D) of this title, only the Medicare+Choice organization shall be entitled to receive payments from the Secretary under this subchapter for services furnished to the individual.

children.heading: FFS payment for expenses for kidney acquisitions
children.label: 3 FFS payment for expenses for kidney acquisitions
children.text: Paragraphs (1) and (2) shall not apply with respect to expenses for organ acquisitions for kidney transplants described in .section 1395w–22(a)(1)(B)(i) of this title

children.heading: Prohibited activities described and limitations on the conduct of certain other activities
children.label: j Prohibited activities described and limitations on the conduct of certain other activities

children.heading: Unsolicited means of direct contact
children.label: A Unsolicited means of direct contact
children.text: Any unsolicited means of direct contact of prospective enrollees, including soliciting door-to-door or any outbound telemarketing without the prospective enrollee initiating contact.

children.heading: Cross-selling
children.label: B Cross-selling
children.text: The sale of other non-health related products (such as annuities and life insurance) during any sales or marketing activity or presentation conducted with respect to a Medicare Advantage plan.

children.heading: Meals
children.label: C Meals
children.text: The provision of meals of any sort, regardless of value, to prospective enrollees at promotional and sales activities.

children.heading: Sales and marketing in health care settings and at educational events
children.label: D Sales and marketing in health care settings and at educational events
i
children.text: in health care settings in areas where health care is delivered to individuals (such as physician offices and pharmacies), except in the case where such activities are conducted in common areas in health care settings; and

children.label: ii
children.text: at educational events.

children.heading: Scope of marketing appointments
children.label: A Scope of marketing appointments
children.text: The scope of any appointment with respect to the marketing of a Medicare Advantage plan. Such limitation shall require advance agreement with a prospective enrollee on the scope of the marketing appointment and documentation of such agreement by the Medicare Advantage organization. In the case where the marketing appointment is in person, such documentation shall be in writing.

children.heading: Co-branding
children.label: B Co-branding
children.text: The use of the name or logo of a co-branded network provider on Medicare Advantage plan membership and marketing materials.

children.heading: Limitation of gifts to nominal dollar value
children.label: C Limitation of gifts to nominal dollar value
children.text: The offering of gifts and other promotional items other than those that are of nominal value (as determined by the Secretary) to prospective enrollees at promotional activities.

children.heading: Compensation
children.label: D Compensation
children.text: The use of compensation other than as provided under guidelines established by the Secretary. Such guidelines shall ensure that the use of compensation creates incentives for agents and brokers to enroll individuals in the Medicare Advantage plan that is intended to best meet their health care needs.

children.heading: Required training, annual retraining, and testing of agents, brokers, and other third parties
children.label: E Required training, annual retraining, and testing of agents, brokers, and other third parties
children.text: The use by a Medicare Advantage organization of any individual as an agent, broker, or other third party representing the organization that has not completed an initial training and testing program and does not complete an annual retraining and testing program.
